origin.question: [µ¢ćõ╗Č 1: 1_s15_15.15.md]15.15. Show that the spacelike slice $\mathbf{v} = \text{constant}(|\mathbf{v}| > 1)$ of the Schwarzschild geometry in Kruskal coordinates $\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}$ cannot be embedded in a Euclidean 3-space. What is the general condition on the slope $\mathrm{dv} / \mathrm{du}$ of a spacelike slice of the Schwarzschild geometry that allows it to be embedded in a Euclidean 3-space?
origin.answer: Õ»╣õ║Ä $|v|>1$’╝īÕĮō $r < 2M \ln v^2$ µŚČÕĄīÕģźÕż▒µĢłŃĆéÕ╣┐õ╣ēÕĄīÕģźµØĪõ╗ČõĖ║ $\frac{r}{2 M} e^{r / 2 M}\left[1-(\mathrm{dv} / \mathrm{du})^{2}\right] \geq\left(\mathrm{u}-\mathrm{v} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{du}}\right)^{2}$ŃĆé
origin.solution: Solution 15.15. In Kruskal coordinates, the Schwarzschild geometry is given by$$\mathrm {d s}^{2} = \frac {3 2 M^{3}}{\mathrm {r}} \mathrm {e}^{- \mathrm {r} / 2 \mathrm {M}} (- \mathrm {d v}^{2} + \mathrm {d u}^{2}) + \mathrm {r}^{2} \mathrm {d} \Omega^{2} \tag {1a}$$where$$\left(\frac {\mathrm {r}}{2 \mathrm {M}} - 1\right) \mathrm {e}^{\mathrm {r} / 2 \mathrm {M}} = \mathrm {u}^{2} - \mathrm {v}^{2}. \tag {1b}$$Setting $\mathbf{v} = \mathbf{\theta}$ constant, and $\theta = \pi /2$ in Equation (1a) and equating the line element to a Euclidean surface of revolution gives$$\frac {3 2 \mathrm {M}^{3}}{\mathrm {r}} \mathrm {e}^{- \mathrm {r} / 2 \mathrm {M}} \mathrm {d u}^{2} + \mathrm {r}^{2} \mathrm {d} \phi^{2} = [ 1 + (\mathrm {d z} / \mathrm {d r})^{2} ] \mathrm {d r}^{2} + \mathrm {r}^{2} \mathrm {d} \phi^{2} \tag {2}$$or$$1 + \left(\frac {\mathrm {d} z}{\mathrm {d r}}\right)^{2} = \frac {3 2 \mathrm {M}^{3}}{\mathrm {r}} \mathrm {e}^{- \mathrm {r} / 2 \mathrm {M}} \left(\frac {\mathrm {d u}}{\mathrm {d r}}\right)^{2}.$$We can calculate du/dr from Equation (1b) to arrive at$$1 + \left(\frac {\mathrm {d} z}{\mathrm {d r}}\right)^{2} = \frac {\mathrm {r}}{2 \mathrm {M}} \frac {\mathrm {e}^{\mathrm {r} / 2 \mathrm {M}}}{(\mathrm {r} / 2 \mathrm {M} - 1) \mathrm {e}^{\mathrm {r} / 2 \mathrm {M}} + \mathrm {v}^{2}}. \tag {3}$$For embedding, $z$ must be a real function, so we must have$$\frac {\mathrm {r} / 2 \mathrm {M} \mathrm {e}^{\mathrm {r} / 2 \mathrm {M}}}{(\mathrm {r} / 2 \mathrm {M} - 1) \mathrm {e}^{\mathrm {r} / 2 \mathrm {M}} + \mathrm {v}^{2}} \geq 1. \tag {4}$$From Equation (4), we conclude$$r / 2 M \geq \log v^{2}.$$Thus, for $|v| > 1$ , there will be a minimum value of $r$ inside which the embedding breaks down.Geometrically, the necessary condition for embedding is that$$\frac {\mathrm {d} (\text {c i r c u m f e r e n c e})}{\mathrm {d} (\text {p r o p e r r a d i u s})} \leq 2 \pi .$$We now consider the general condition on dv/du which allows Euclidean embedding: Suppose we have a slice described by $\mathbf{v}(\mathbf{u})$ . Then$$\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {d s}^{2} = \frac {3 2 \mathrm {M}^{3}}{\mathrm {r}} \mathrm {e}^{- \mathrm {r} / 2 \mathrm {M}} \left[ 1 - \left(\frac {\mathrm {d v}}{\mathrm {d u}}\right)^{2}\right] \mathrm {d u}^{2} + \mathrm {r}^{2} \mathrm {d} \phi^{2} \tag {6} \\ = \frac {3 2 M^{3}}{r} e^{- r / 2 M} \left[ 1 - \left(\frac {d v}{d u}\right)^{2}\right] \left[ \frac {(r / 8 M^{2}) e^{r / 2 M}}{(u - v \frac {d v}{d u})}\right]^{2} d r^{2} + r^{2} d \phi^{2} \\ \end{array}$$and we require$$\frac {\mathrm {r}}{2 \mathrm {M}} \mathrm {e}^{\mathrm {r} / 2 \mathrm {M}} \left[ 1 - \left(\frac {\mathrm {d v}}{\mathrm {d u}}\right)^{2}\right] \geq \left(\mathrm {u} - \mathrm {v} \frac {\mathrm {d v}}{\mathrm {d u}}\right)^{2} \tag {7}$$at each r.
